mnemonics: DOM TREE: Document Object Model is a Tree of nodes representing the page. Think of it as a family tree for your HTML.
keyPoints: The DOM is a programming interface for HTML and XML documents, representing the structure as a tree of objects.
JavaScript can be used to manipulate the DOM, allowing you to change document structure, style, and content dynamically.
Common methods for selecting elements include getElementById, getElementsByClassName, getElementsByTagName, and querySelector.
commonMistakes: Trying to manipulate an element before it has loaded in the DOM, resulting in a 'null' error. Always ensure scripts run after the DOM is ready.
Inefficiently selecting elements, such as using a broad tag selector inside a loop, which can cause performance issues on large pages.
